title: Gladys Assistant external integration manifest
description: Schema of the `gladys-assistant-integration.json` file placed at the root of an integration repository (manifest_version 1). This file is the canonical source: the store indexer and the Gladys server both validate manifests against it. Some rules cannot be expressed in JSON Schema and are enforced by code: `version` must be strict semver, `gladys_version` must be a valid semver range (npm syntax), `docker_image` must be a well-formed image reference with an explicit tag or digest (main image and sub-containers alike), `config_schema` keys, `contact_schema` keys, action keys and webhook keys must be unique, defaults must match their field type and are not allowed on dynamic-`source` selects, sub-container names must be unique, sub-container `env` cannot use reserved GLADYS_* keys and volume paths cannot contain `..` segments. The repository must also ship the mandatory user documentation (`docs/en.md` and `docs/fr.md`, at least 300 characters each), checked by the indexer outside of this schema.

manifest_version.description: Version of the manifest format. Only 1 is supported: a manifest with a higher version is rejected by indexers and servers that do not support it.

type.description: Integration type. "device" integrations publish discovered devices; "communication" integrations (messaging channels, contract B.15) exchange messages for Gladys users and only expose the Configuration screen — their family (bidirectional chat channel or send-only notification channel) is declared by the mandatory `messaging` field.

name.description: Display name, used as the title of the catalog card.

description.description: Short multi-language description displayed on the catalog card. The `en` key is mandatory, other languages are optional.

version.description: Version of the integration. Must be strict semver (e.g. "1.2.0"); bumping it is what triggers the "update available" flag in Gladys.

docker_image.description: Docker image reference on any public registry, with an explicit tag or digest (e.g. "ghcr.io/john/gladys-open-meteo-demo:1.2.0"). The image must exist on its registry and be anonymously pullable: the indexer verifies it against the registry API.

gladys_version.description: Range of compatible Gladys versions, npm semver range syntax (e.g. ">=4.62.0"). Used by Gladys to filter the catalog.

cover_image.description: HTTPS URL of a JPEG or PNG cover image, exactly 800x534 pixels, 150 KB max. Validated then re-hosted by the indexer; a missing or invalid cover does not reject the integration (a placeholder is used and a warning is published).

config_schema.description: Flat list of configuration fields, rendered as a generated form by the Gladys UI (declarative UI: no code injection).

containers.description: Authorization contract of the additional sub-containers (multi-container integrations, e.g. Frigate + Mosquitto): everything that can run — images, limits, published ports, hardware access — shown to the user on the install screen. The lifecycle is then driven by the integration through the /container host API, only within these bounds.

network_discovery.description: Curated network capture requests (mediated discovery): the Gladys core, running in network=host, captures on behalf of the integration, which interprets the raw results itself. Shown on the install screen as an authorization contract; no arbitrary capture.

actions.description: On-demand operations rendered as buttons in the Configuration screen (connection test, identify, protocol detection...). Each action is relayed to the integration over WebSocket and acked within its own declared timeout.

transports.description: Channels the integration knows how to use. When both are declared, Gladys renders the standard core-translated "Prefer local (LAN) connection" toggle, stored as the reserved GLADYS_PREFER_LOCAL config key (boolean, default true) — readable by the integration, never writable by it. The preference is a wish; the per-device reality is reported through the GLADYS_TRANSPORT param.

messaging.description: Family of a communication channel (contract B.15) — mandatory when `type` is "communication", forbidden otherwise. Sending is always present, receiving is not; the linking model, the endpoints and the install-screen warning level all follow from `receive`.

receive.description: true: bidirectional chat channel (Telegram-like bot) — users link their account with a short code sent in the channel, and incoming messages carry the linked user's authority. false: send-only notification channel (Free Mobile/CallMeBot-like) — no incoming path exists (the host API guarantees it server-side), each user enters their own credentials in the "My account" block described by `contact_schema`.

contact_schema.description: Per-user identity of a send-only notification channel (contract B.15) — mandatory when `messaging.receive` is false, forbidden otherwise. Same flat field format and rules as `config_schema`; rendered as the "My account" block of the Configuration screen, where each user enters their own values (stored scoped to the user, passed to the integration with every outgoing message).

webhooks.description: Incoming webhooks relayed by Gladys Plus (contract B.17): third parties push events from the Internet to a public per-webhook URL, and Gladys Plus relays them to the integration — shown on the install screen ("may receive events from the Internet through Gladys Plus"). The user pastes their Gladys Plus Open API key in the standard "Gladys Plus webhooks" block of the Configuration screen; the integration fetches the ready-to-register URLs through the host API.

sectionText.description: Plain multi-language text of a `section` field: at most 1000 characters per language, no markdown nor HTML interpreted (declarative UI). The long step-by-step guide belongs to the mandatory repository documentation (docs/en.md, docs/fr.md).

key.description: Unique key of the field, stored in Gladys variables scoped to the integration.

type.description: Field widget type. `secret` values are never sent back to the frontend; `oauth2` renders a Connect button (relayed OAuth2 authorization-code flow, tokens stored off-schema by the integration); `multi_select` renders checkboxes and stores an array of option values; `section` is a purely presentational intro block (title, plain text, https links) that stores no value.

default.description: Default value. Must match the field type (code-enforced); not allowed for `secret` and `oauth2` fields.

source.description: Dynamic options of a `select` or `multi_select` field, provided by the Gladys core (mutually exclusive with `options`) — a reserved enum, never a URL nor an expression. `devices`: the options are the already-created devices of the integration (label = device name, value = external_id).

display.description: Rendering of a `select` field: dropdown (default) or radio buttons.

links.description: Links of a `section` field, opened in a new tab with the target domain shown next to the label.

url.description: https URL of the link (http is refused).

name.description: Unique name of the sub-container: DNS alias on the private network of the integration, suffix of the container name and identifier in the /container host API.

docker_image.description: Docker image reference, same rules as the main image (public registry, explicit tag or digest, multi-arch recommended).

start.description: `auto`: created and started by the supervisor before the main container. `manual`: nothing starts until the integration calls POST /container/:name/start (e.g. generate a password file in /data first).

env.description: Static string environment variables. GLADYS_* keys are forbidden (code-enforced). The manifest is public: never a secret here — runtime credentials go through /data files or the env of POST /container/:name/start.

volumes.description: Absolute container paths. Each one is mounted from a sub-folder derived from the integration data folder by the supervisor — the manifest never chooses a host path.

ports.description: Published ports. The host port is chosen by Gladys (free, persisted across recreations), never declared. Publishing a port exposes a third-party interface on the LAN: it is shown on the install screen.

label.description: Names the "Open" link in the Gladys UI.

devices.description: Requested hardware access classes — never a free /dev path. Requesting is not obtaining: each class is granted or refused by the user in the UI; the effective mount is requested ∩ granted ∩ present.

read_only.description: Read-only root filesystem. Opt-out possible: some upstream images (e.g. Frigate) do not run with a read-only rootfs.

memory_mb.description: Memory limit in MB, shown on the install screen.

cpu.description: CPU limit, shown on the install screen.

shm_mb.description: /dev/shm size in MB, useful for video processing.

command.description: Optional override of the image CMD.

type.description: Capture type: passive listen on declared UDP broadcast ports, active broadcast query/response (the core broadcasts an integration-forged payload on a declared port and relays the unicast replies — the TP-Link Kasa case), mDNS browse of a declared service type, or SSDP M-SEARCH on a declared st.

ports.description: UDP broadcast ports to listen on (`udp-broadcast`) or allowed as active broadcast destinations (`udp-active-broadcast`); these two types only.

service.description: DNS-SD service type to browse (e.g. "_hue._tcp"), `mdns` type only.

st.description: SSDP search target for the M-SEARCH, `ssdp` type only.

key.description: Unique key of the webhook: last segment of the public relay URL and identifier in the host API and SDK handlers.

label.description: Names the webhook in the install screen and in the "Gladys Plus webhooks" block.

mode.description: `fire_and_forget` (default): the third party only awaits an acknowledgment — Gladys answers immediately and relays asynchronously (the Netatmo-style event stream). `sync`: the caller awaits the integration response (challenge/response registrations, Strava/Microsoft Graph style) — the handler's resolved { status (200-499), contentType, body (≤ 64 KB) } is returned verbatim through Gladys Plus.

timeout_seconds.description: Ack delay granted to the action (exception to the 5s command rule: these operations can be long).

fields.description: Optional flat form shown before running the action, same format as config_schema entries.
